- Node 13: color=W, pixels=60, boundary=[[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 31], [42, 27]] - white region in bottom block
- Node 14: color=p, pixels=40, boundary=[[39, 31], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - purple region in bottom block
The bottom block has white (60 px) and purple (40 px). The target is white (50 px) and purple (50 px).
The white region is 10 px too many, and the purple region is 10 px too few.
Looking at the boundaries:
- White: [[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 31], [42, 27]] - this is an irregular shape covering the top portion and extending down the left side
- Purple: [[39, 31], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - this is a rectangle at the bottom-right (rows 39-43, cols 27-36)
The purple region is 5 rows x 10 cols = 50 px, but the count is 40 px. So it's not a full rectangle.
Wait, the boundary [[39, 31], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] suggests:
- Top edge: row 39, cols 31-36
- Right edge: col 36, rows 39-43
- Bottom edge: row 43, cols 27-36
- Left edge: col 27, rows 39-43
But the top edge starts at col 31, not col 27. So the purple region is a trapezoid or L-shape.
Actually, looking at the boundary more carefully, it's a clockwise perimeter:
- [39, 31] -> [39, 36] -> [43, 36] -> [43, 27] -> back to [39, 31]
This is a rectangle with corners at (39, 31), (39, 36), (43, 36), and (43, 27). But the left edge goes from (43, 27) to (39, 31), which is a diagonal, not a vertical line. So the shape is a trapezoid.
The white region has boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 31], [42, 27]], which is also an irregular shape.
The issue is that the stamping from different positions produces overlapping coverage, and the result is not a clean horizontal split.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to stamp from multiple positions, let me try to stamp from a single position that produces the correct pattern.
Actually, I realize that the target pattern is white on top (50 px) and purple on bottom (50 px). The bottom block is 10x10, so the top half is rows 34-38 (5 rows) and the bottom half is rows 39-43 (5 rows).
Currently, the white region covers rows 34-38 (top half) plus some additional rows on the left side. The purple region covers rows 39-43 (bottom half) but only on the right side.
To get the correct pattern, I need to:

2. Make the bottom half (rows 39-43) all purple
Currently, the top half is mostly white, but the bottom-left corner of the top half might have some purple. And the bottom half is mostly purple, but the bottom-left corner might have some white.
Looking at the white boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 31], [42, 27]], the white region extends from row 34 to row 42, which includes the entire top half (rows 34-38) and part of the bottom half (rows 39-42).
The purple boundary [[39, 31], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] shows that the purple region is in the bottom-right corner, from row 39 to row 43, cols 27-36.
So the bottom block has:
- Top half (rows 34-38): mostly white, with some purple in the bottom-right
- Bottom half (rows 39-43): mostly purple, with some white in the bottom-left
This is not the target pattern. I need to make the top half all white and the bottom half all purple.
Let me try stamping purple from the below position. The block is currently at the above position. I need to navigate to the below position and stamp purple.
From above: RIGHT -> right -> DOWN -> below
Then select purple and press SPACE.
But wait, I already tried this earlier and it didn't produce the correct result. The issue is that the below stamp (diamond shape) doesn't cover the entire bottom half.
Let me try a different approach. Let me stamp white from the right position to make the right half white. Then the bottom block would have:

- Right half: white (from right stamp)
- Bottom-left: purple (remaining)
This would result in a bottom block with white in the top and right, and purple in the bottom-left. This is not the target pattern either.
I think the issue is that the stamping mechanic doesn't allow me to produce a clean horizontal split. The stamps from different positions overlap and produce irregular patterns.
Let me try yet another approach. Let me reset the bottom block to all white by stamping white from all three positions, then stamp purple from the below position.

2. RIGHT -> right -> SPACE (stamp white from right)
3. DOWN -> below -> SPACE (stamp white from below)
4. Select purple
5. SPACE (stamp purple from below)
This should make the bottom block all white, then stamp purple on the bottom half.
But this is a lot of actions. Let me try a shorter sequence.
Actually, let me first check if the bottom block is already close to the target. The current state has white (60 px) and purple (40 px). The target is white (50 px) and purple (50 px). The difference is 10 px.
If I stamp purple from the below position, it might add 10 more purple pixels to the bottom half, making it 50 px purple. Let me try that.
